creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
I'm: +1 the *un*-prefixed usernames, for the MAINTAINER=<barename> reason, reduced complexity/cases for auto-assigner, and because accounts can have a 'realname' for user friendliness, that can also serve to clarify that something is or isn't a mailing list (if necessary).

I think the latter is handy in the non-autoassigned-case if/when someone doesn't want to spam a list. Seeing 'mailing list' in the realname on autocompleting the address can prevent doing that, either accidentally or otherwise.
